generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingHVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for HVAC systems within BIM environment, configured by Building Control Engineers via MDE-driven transformations. Supports remote control interfaces through interactive visualization tools like Unreal Engine for Facility Managers."
]

instance StructuralHealthActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for structural health monitoring systems, integrated into BIM environment to enable adaptive control logic for building infrastructure. Deployed as executable artifacts via model-driven engineering."
]

instance PlumbingSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for plumbing subsystems within AECO infrastructure, configured through BIM tools and deployed via MDE transformations for real-time operational control."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic environmental temperature sensor modeled within BIM environment by Building Control Engineers. Part of real-time data acquisition infrastructure for Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic structural health sensor integrated into BIM model for real-time monitoring of building infrastructure. Data extracted via MDE transformations for Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancyStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic occupancy status sensor modeled in BIM environment to capture real-time occupancy data for building operations. Enables dynamic adaptation of building systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic energy consumption sensor integrated into BIM model for real-time energy monitoring. Supports data-driven decision making for energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

instance OccupancyStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancyStatusSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model derived from BIM representing physical building structures. Provides spatial context for Digital Twin operations and serves as foundation for all other model types."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance BIMBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model defined by Building Control Engineers encoding control logic for devices (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM environment. Creates semantic foundation for subsequent MDE transformations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance BIMAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model generated via MDE principles extracting structured representations from enriched BIM models. Enables automated generation of deployable Digital Twin artifacts like executable models and simulation scripts."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance DeviceSpecificData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured representations of Building Control System components (sensors, actuators, controllers) extracted from BIM. Ensures alignment between physical and digital assets through semantic consistency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, StructuralHealthDataTransmitted, OccupancyStatusDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized operational data between physical building and Digital Twin via real-time data exchange capabilities. Enables dynamic updates to the twin's state with low-latency, event-driven updates."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, StructuralHealthDataTransmitted, OccupancyStatusDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework rooted in BIM addressing static limitations by integrating dynamic operational representations. Supports multi-faceted computational models through automated transformations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight M2M service for secure, scalable IoT data ingestion enabling real-time connectivity between physical sensors and Digital Twin. Provides protocol-mediated communication for data exchange."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin framework for device management and data modeling providing abstraction layers to unify heterogeneous data sources. Supports digital twin entanglement through standardized data models."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity, interactive 3D visualization engine integrating real-time data streams into immersive Digital Twin environments. Enables intuitive analysis and what-if scenario testing."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, DecisionSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously ingests and processes runtime data (sensor readings, environmental metrics, structural health indicators) to generate dynamic virtual replicas of physical assets. Enables real-time simulation of system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight, OperationalOptimizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identifies failure risks and degradation patterns through data-driven analytics to support predictive maintenance across building lifecycle. Provides actionable insights for proactive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailureRiskInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance LifeCycleOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports holistic optimization across entire building lifecycle from design phase trade-offs to operational efficiency improvements and refurbishment planning. Facilitates data-driven decision making for infrastructure management."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables DT Engineers to develop and integrate custom services and plugins to extend analytics capabilities. Supports predictive maintenance, fault detection, and adaptive control algorithms through modular architecture."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight, AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers rich, immersive visualizations integrating 3D BIM models with real-time data overlays. Supports multi-dimensional data exploration including spatial, temporal, and performance-based metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualSystemStateInsight, ScenarioTestingAction
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides actionable insights for Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers to quantify ROI for interventions. Enables collaborative decision-making through shared interactive visualizations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ROIQuantificationInsight, CollaborativeDecisionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insights into building operations including sensor data, actuator statuses, and controller performance. Enables proactive maintenance and anomaly detection for Facility Managers."
]

instance OperationalOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insights for optimizing building control logic, energy efficiency, and infrastructure utilization based on synchronized physical-digital data. Supports data-driven operational improvements."
]

instance FailureRiskInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified failure risks and degradation patterns through predictive analytics. Provides quantitative assessment of component health and remaining useful life."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for maintenance tasks prioritized based on failure risk analysis and operational impact. Supports proactive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative analysis of energy consumption patterns and optimization opportunities across building systems. Supports data-driven energy management decisions."
]

instance RefurbishmentRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for building refurbishment interventions based on lifecycle analysis and current operational performance. Includes cost-benefit assessments for proposed upgrades."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results from custom analytics services integrated into the Digital Twin framework. Includes predictive maintenance models, fault detection algorithms, and adaptive control strategies."
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to building control systems based on real-time data and predictive models. Enables dynamic response to changing operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization of current system state including all real-time sensor data and operational metrics. Provides spatial and temporal context for building operations."
]

instance ScenarioTestingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of what-if scenarios to evaluate potential system configurations or operational changes. Enables data-driven decision making before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ROIQuantificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of return on investment for proposed interventions including energy retrofits and system upgrades. Provides financial justification for capital expenditures."
]

instance CollaborativeDecisionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facilitation of collaborative decision-making through shared Digital Twin visualizations and insights. Bridges gaps between technical and non-technical stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C5: Environment
instance AECOBuildingInfrastructureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous infrastructure environment integrating mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and structural subsystems within AECO domain. Supports complex building operations across lifecycle phases."
    base:contains BuildingHVACActuator, StructuralHealthActuator, PlumbingSystemActuator,
                  EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor, StructuralHealthSensor, OccupancyStatusSensor,
                  EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT framework enables **control flows** from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed by **Facility Managers** via an immersive, interactive interface. Commands are transmitted as **structured control messages**, validated against operational constraints (e.g., safety thresholds, system limits) before execution. The framework supports **bidirectional communication** for feedback loops, where physical systems (e.g., lighting, HVAC) acknowledge command receipt and report execution status. While the document does not specify exact validation/authentication mechanisms or conflict resolution protocols, the system is designed to integrate with **custom services and analytics** (via standardized extension points), allowing for dynamic adaptation to operational needs. The **runtime configuration management** feature ensures that control logic can be adjusted without full redeployment, maintaining system responsiveness. Emergency overrides or priority-based command handling are implied by the framework’s emphasis on **real-time monitoring and control**, though specific implementation details are not provided in the document."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms, or event-driven triggers), the requirement implies **low-latency operational needs** for monitoring and control, particularly for **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed Digital Twin. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to synchronization parameters without full redeployment, though no explicit latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) or temporal granularity (e.g., sub-second, hourly) are defined. The system bridges **static BIM representations** (typically updated during design/construction phases) with **dynamic operational data**, suggesting a hybrid time-scale approach where historical BIM data is enriched with real-time sensor/actuator feeds. No explicit mention of discrete time scales for different services (e.g., HVAC vs. structural monitoring) is provided, but the modular architecture implies **service-specific temporal requirements** could be configured post-deployment."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based constellation architecture**, where the **DT Framework** acts as a **central orchestrator** integrating **enriched BIM models**, **real-time operational data**, and **custom services** into a unified life-cycle management system. The architecture follows a **modular pattern** with **standardized extension points**, enabling **loose coupling** between components (e.g., BIM tools, monitoring dashboards, analytics services). **Resource management** is implied through **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to service integration without full redeployment, though no specific **orchestration protocols** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Kafka) or **resource allocation strategies** (e.g., QoS policies) are mentioned. The constellation supports **holistic building life-cycle management** by linking **static design data** (BIM) with **dynamic operational needs** (AECO domain), with **Facility Managers** and **DT Engineers** collaborating via the framework’s bidirectional data flow. No explicit mention of **microservices**, **event-driven coordination**, or **distributed ledger** mechanisms is provided, but the emphasis on **interoperability** and **scalability** suggests a **service-oriented architecture (SOA)** with **event-based triggers** for cross-component synchronization."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, encompassing the following phases: 

1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define and configure Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers) directly within the BIM environment, leveraging enriched BIM models to create a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase addresses BIM’s static limitations by enabling dynamic BCS representation.

2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Using **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., runtime models, service configurations) with minimal manual intervention. This ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.

3. **DT Deployment and Synchronization**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, ensuring continuous synchronization. This phase includes the operationalization of control logic and the integration of physical infrastructure components.

4. **Runtime Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and operate physical infrastructure via the twin. This phase emphasizes **immersive visualization** and **interactive interfaces** for real-time oversight.

5. **Custom Service Integration and Evolution**: DT Engineers extend DT functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** through standardized extension points. This modular architecture enables the DT to evolve with changing operational requirements, supporting **runtime extensibility** and **dynamic adaptation**.

**Transition Management**: The framework ensures smooth transitions between phases via **automated model transformations** (MDE) and **standardized interfaces** for data exchange and service integration. **Lifecycle Governance** is implied through role-based access (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and the modular, extensible architecture, which enforces consistency and traceability across phases."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **fidelity** through **real-time data exchange capabilities**, ensuring continuous synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported by the framework’s **automated artifact generation** and **semantic transformations**, which align the DT’s behavioral and control models with the physical system. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** quantifies how well the DT reflects its physical counterpart, serving as a **quality assurance process** for model fidelity. However, the documents do not specify **uncertainty sources**, **error detection/correction mechanisms**, or **quantitative validation methods** beyond the ODTE metric. **Real-time synchronization** is the primary mechanism for maintaining data integrity, while the **modular architecture** allows for extensibility to include additional validation tools."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with a **centralized DT Framework hub** to orchestrate data and control flows. Communication relies on **standardized middleware** (e.g., Eclipse Hono and Ditto, as inferred from related contexts) to enable **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**. The network topology is **hub-and-spoke**, with the DT Framework acting as the central node for bidirectional data exchange. While the document does not specify exact **communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) or **network topology details** (e.g., mesh, star), the system is designed to support **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. **Reliability and redundancy** are addressed through the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize maintainability and low-overhead updates. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s alignment with **interoperability standards** suggests adherence to industry best practices (e.g., encrypted communication channels)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability** with external systems through standardized protocols and connectivity solutions. While the document does not explicitly name specific integration patterns (e.g., REST, MQTT), it references **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as connectivity technologies for enabling seamless data exchange. The framework supports integration with **heterogeneous systems**, including BIM tools (e.g., for device modeling by *Building Control Engineers*), and adheres to **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** addressing scalability, maintainability, and accessibility. The system’s modular architecture allows for **dependency management** with external enterprise systems or other Digital Twins, though explicit protocols or standards (e.g., IFC, OPC UA) are not detailed. The focus is on **bidirectional data flow** and **operational synchronization** between physical assets and digital representations, ensuring alignment with dynamic AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain needs."
]
